title: Constructive set theory
body:
Axiomatic constructive set theory is an approach to mathematical constructivism following the program of axiomatic set theory.
The same first-order language with "
  
    
      
        =
      
    
    {\displaystyle =}
  
" and "
  
    
      
        ∈
      
    
    {\displaystyle \in }
  
" of classical set theory is usually used, so this is not to be confused with a constructive types approach.
On the other hand, some constructive theories are indeed motivated by their interpretability in type theories.
In addition to rejecting the principle of excluded middle (
  
    
      
        
          
            P
            E
            M
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle {\mathrm {PEM} }}
  
), constructive set theories often require some logical quantifiers in their axioms to be set bounded. The latter is motivated by results tied to impredicativity.
